methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Sublime Systems is a Series B-stage climate and energy technology company founded by Leah Ellis and Yet-Ming Chiang that has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation analysis relies on a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public (LNZA) and eight private—operating within the same sector and stage, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base of $80M and applying a typical 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $152M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $355M, with the peer set ranging from $120M to $5.0B. This wide dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in the climate-tech landscape at Series B maturity.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $129M to $408M, with a median of $254M. The stage-progression method yields the lower bound, while peer-median anchors the upper end. Key uncertainties include capital intensity and commercialization timeline relative to peer cohorts—particularly given the $5.0B outlier (Commonwealth Fusion Systems) versus the $300M–$500M cluster. Without clarity on Sublime's specific technological differentiation and go-to-market momentum relative to peers, the median estimate of $254M represents the most defensible central case.
